ClientRequest.title: ClientRequest
ClientRequest.description: Request from the client to the server.

oneOf.description: NEW APIs
oneOf.title: Thread/startRequest

oneOf.description:
Increment the thread-local out-of-band elicitation counter.

This is used by external helpers to pause timeout accounting while a user approval or other elicitation is pending outside the app-server request flow.
oneOf.title: Thread/incrementElicitationRequest

oneOf.description:
Decrement the thread-local out-of-band elicitation counter.

When the count reaches zero, timeout accounting resumes for the thread.
oneOf.title: Thread/decrementElicitationRequest

oneOf.description: Append raw Responses API items to the thread history without starting a user turn.
oneOf.title: Thread/injectItemsRequest

oneOf.description: Lists collaboration mode presets.
oneOf.title: CollaborationMode/listRequest

oneOf.description: Test-only method used to validate experimental gating.
oneOf.title: Mock/experimentalMethodRequest

oneOf.description: Adds or replaces a remote environment by id for later selection.
oneOf.title: Environment/addRequest

oneOf.description: Reads information from a configured execution environment.
oneOf.title: Environment/infoRequest

oneOf.description: Execute a standalone command (argv vector) under the server's sandbox.
oneOf.title: Command/execRequest

oneOf.description: Write stdin bytes to a running `command/exec` session or close stdin.
oneOf.title: Command/exec/writeRequest

oneOf.description: Terminate a running `command/exec` session by client-supplied `processId`.
oneOf.title: Command/exec/terminateRequest

oneOf.description: Resize a running PTY-backed `command/exec` session by client-supplied `processId`.
oneOf.title: Command/exec/resizeRequest

oneOf.description: Spawn a standalone process (argv vector) without a Codex sandbox.
oneOf.title: Process/spawnRequest

oneOf.description: Write stdin bytes to a running `process/spawn` session or close stdin.
oneOf.title: Process/writeStdinRequest

oneOf.description: Terminate a running `process/spawn` session by client-supplied `processHandle`.

oneOf.description: Resize a running PTY-backed `process/spawn` session by client-supplied `processHandle`.
oneOf.title: Process/resizePtyRequest

network.description: Partial overlay used for per-command permission requests.

callId.description: Use to correlate this with [codex_protocol::protocol::PatchApplyBeginEvent] and [codex_protocol::protocol::PatchApplyEndEvent].

grantRoot.description: When set, the agent is asking the user to allow writes under this root for the remainder of the session (unclear if this is honored today).

reason.description: Optional explanatory reason (e.g. request for extra write access).

previousAccountId.description:
Workspace/account identifier that Codex was previously using.

Clients that manage multiple accounts/workspaces can use this as a hint to refresh the token for the correct workspace.

This may be `null` when the prior auth state did not include a workspace identifier (`chatgpt_account_id`).

oneOf.description: Codex attempted a backend request and received `401 Unauthorized`.
User approved the command.
User approved the command and future prompts in the same session-scoped approval cache should run without prompting.
User approved the command, and wants to apply the proposed execpolicy amendment so future matching commands can run without prompting.
oneOf.title: AcceptWithExecpolicyAmendmentCommandExecutionApprovalDecision

oneOf.description: User chose a persistent network policy rule (allow/deny) for this host.
oneOf.title: ApplyNetworkPolicyAmendmentCommandExecutionApprovalDecision

oneOf.description: User denied the command. The agent will continue the turn.
User denied the command. The turn will also be immediately interrupted.

additionalPermissions.description: Optional additional permissions requested for this command.

approvalId.description:
Unique identifier for this specific approval callback.

For regular shell/unified_exec approvals, this is null.

For zsh-exec-bridge subcommand approvals, multiple callbacks can belong to one parent `itemId`, so `approvalId` is a distinct opaque callback id (a UUID) used to disambiguate routing.

availableDecisions.description: Ordered list of decisions the client may present for this prompt.

commandActions.description: Best-effort parsed command actions for friendly display.

environmentId.description: Environment in which the command will run.

networkApprovalContext.description: Optional context for a managed-network approval prompt.

proposedExecpolicyAmendment.description: Optional proposed execpolicy amendment to allow similar commands without prompting.

proposedNetworkPolicyAmendments.description: Optional proposed network policy amendments (allow/deny host) for future requests.

reason.description: Optional explanatory reason (e.g. request for network access).

startedAtMs.description: Unix timestamp (in milliseconds) when this approval request started.

approvalId.description: Identifier for this specific approval callback.

callId.description: Use to correlate this with [codex_protocol::protocol::ExecCommandBeginEvent] and [codex_protocol::protocol::ExecCommandEndEvent].

grantRoot.description: [UNSTABLE] When set, the agent is asking the user to allow writes under this root for the remainder of the session (unclear if this is honored today).

reason.description: Optional explanatory reason (e.g. request for extra write access).

startedAtMs.description: Unix timestamp (in milliseconds) when this approval request started.

McpElicitationSchema.description:
Typed form schema for MCP `elicitation/create` requests.

This matches the `requestedSchema` shape from the MCP 2025-11-25 `ElicitRequestFormParams` schema.

turnId.description:
Active Codex turn when this elicitation was observed, if app-server could correlate one.

This is nullable because MCP models elicitation as a standalone server-to-client request identified by the MCP server request id. It may be triggered during a turn, but turn context is app-server correlation rather than part of the protocol identity of the elicitation itself.

path.description: (Best effort) Path to the file being read by the command. When possible, this is an absolute path, though when relative, it should be resolved against the `cwd`` that will be used to run the command to derive the absolute path.

startedAtMs.description: Unix timestamp (in milliseconds) when this approval request started.

AbsolutePathBuf.description:
A path that is guaranteed to be absolute and normalized (though it is not guaranteed to be canonicalized or exist on the filesystem).

IMPORTANT: When deserializing an `AbsolutePathBuf`, a base path must be set using [AbsolutePathBufGuard::new]. If no base path is set, the deserialization will fail unless the path being deserialized is already absolute.

ApprovalsReviewer.description: Configures who approval requests are routed to for review. Examples include sandbox escapes, blocked network access, MCP approval prompts, and ARC escalations. Defaults to `user`. `auto_review` uses a carefully prompted subagent to gather relevant context and apply a risk-based decision framework before approving or denying the request. The legacy value `guardian_subagent` is accepted for compatibility.

CapabilityRootLocation.description: Location used to resolve a selected capability root.

oneOf.description: A path owned by an execution environment.

path.description: Absolute path for the root in the selected environment.

oneOf.title: EnvironmentCapabilityRootLocation

MultiAgentMode.description: Controls the effective multi-agent delegation instructions for a turn. `custom` means the configured mode hint defines the policy instead of a built-in policy.

SelectedCapabilityRoot.description: A user-selected root that can expose one or more runtime capabilities.

id.description: Stable identifier supplied by the capability selection platform.

location.description: Where the selected root can be resolved.

allowProviderModelFallback.description: Allow a provider with an authoritative static model catalog to replace an unavailable requested model with its default.

approvalsReviewer.description: Override where approval requests are routed for review on this thread and subsequent turns.

environments.description:
Optional sticky environments for this thread.

Omitted selects the default environment when environment access is enabled. Empty disables environment access for turns that do not provide a turn override. Non-empty selects the first environment as the current turn environment.

experimentalRawEvents.description: If true, opt into emitting raw Responses API items on the event stream. This is for internal use only (e.g. Codex Cloud).

historyMode.description: Persisted thread history contract to use for this new thread.

mockExperimentalField.description: Test-only experimental field used to validate experimental gating and schema filtering behavior in a stable way.

multiAgentMode.description: @deprecated Ignored. Use Ultra reasoning effort for proactive multi-agent behavior.

permissions.description: Named profile id for this thread. Cannot be combined with `sandbox`.

runtimeWorkspaceRoots.description: Replace the thread's runtime workspace roots. Paths must be absolute.

selectedCapabilityRoots.description: Capability roots selected for this thread by the hosting platform.

threadSource.description: Optional client-supplied analytics source classification for this thread.

FunctionCallOutputContentItem.description: Responses API compatible content items that can be returned by a tool call. This is a subset of ContentItem with the types we support as function call outputs.

InternalChatMessageMetadataPassthrough.description:
Internal Responses API passthrough metadata copied into underlying chat messages.

Responses API strongly types this payload. Do not modify it without first getting API approval and making the corresponding Responses API change.

MessagePhase.description:
Classifies an assistant message as interim commentary or final answer text.

Providers do not emit this consistently, so callers must treat `None` as "phase unknown" and keep compatibility behavior for legacy models.

oneOf.description:
Mid-turn assistant text (for example preamble/progress narration).

Additional tool calls or assistant output may follow before turn completion.
The assistant's terminal answer text for the current turn.

call_id.description: Set when using the Responses API.

id.description: Legacy id field retained for compatibility with older payloads.

itemsView.description: How much item detail to include for each returned turn; defaults to summary.

sortDirection.description: Optional turn pagination direction; defaults to descending.

oneOf.description: `items` was not loaded for this turn. The field is intentionally empty.
`items` contains only a display summary for this turn.
`items` contains every ThreadItem available from persisted app-server history for this turn.

ThreadResumeParams.title: ThreadResumeParams
ThreadResumeParams.description:
There are three ways to resume a thread: 1. By thread_id: load the thread from disk by thread_id and resume it. 2. By history: instantiate the thread from memory and resume it. 3. By path: load the thread from disk by path and resume it.

For non-running threads, the precedence is: history > non-empty path > thread_id. If using history or a non-empty path for a non-running thread, the thread_id param will be ignored.

If thread_id identifies a running thread, app-server rejoins that thread and treats a non-empty path as a consistency check against the active rollout path. Empty string path values are treated as absent.

Prefer using thread_id whenever possible.

approvalsReviewer.description: Override where approval requests are routed for review on this thread and subsequent turns.

excludeTurns.description: When true, return only thread metadata and live-resume state without populating `thread.turns`. This is useful when the client plans to call `thread/turns/list` immediately after resuming.

history.description: [UNSTABLE] FOR CODEX CLOUD - DO NOT USE. If specified, the thread will be resumed with the provided history instead of loaded from disk.

initialTurnsPage.description: When present, include a `thread/turns/list` page in the resume response so clients can bootstrap recent turns without a second request.

model.description: Configuration overrides for the resumed thread, if any.

path.description: [UNSTABLE] Specify the rollout path to resume from. If specified for a non-running thread, the thread_id param will be ignored. If thread_id identifies a running thread, the path must match the active rollout path.

permissions.description: Named profile id for the resumed thread. Cannot be combined with `sandbox`.

runtimeWorkspaceRoots.description: Replace the thread's runtime workspace roots. Paths must be absolute.

ThreadForkParams.title: ThreadForkParams
ThreadForkParams.description:
There are two ways to fork a thread: 1. By thread_id: load the thread from disk by thread_id and fork it into a new thread. 2. By path: load the thread from disk by path and fork it into a new thread.

If using a non-empty path, the thread_id param will be ignored. Empty string path values are treated as absent.

Prefer using thread_id whenever possible.

approvalsReviewer.description: Override where approval requests are routed for review on this thread and subsequent turns.

excludeTurns.description: When true, return only thread metadata and live fork state without populating `thread.turns`. This is useful when the client plans to call `thread/turns/list` immediately after forking.

lastTurnId.description:
Optional last turn id to fork through, inclusive.

When specified, turns after `last_turn_id` are omitted from the fork. The referenced turn cannot be in progress.

model.description: Configuration overrides for the forked thread, if any.

path.description: [UNSTABLE] Specify the rollout path to fork from. If specified, the thread_id param will be ignored.

permissions.description: Named profile id for the forked thread. Cannot be combined with `sandbox`.

runtimeWorkspaceRoots.description: Replace the thread's runtime workspace roots. Paths must be absolute.

threadSource.description: Optional client-supplied analytics source classification for this forked thread.

threadId.description: Thread whose out-of-band elicitation counter should be incremented.
Thread whose out-of-band elicitation counter should be decremented.

branch.description: Omit to leave the stored branch unchanged, set to `null` to clear it, or provide a non-empty string to replace it.

originUrl.description: Omit to leave the stored origin URL unchanged, set to `null` to clear it, or provide a non-empty string to replace it.

sha.description: Omit to leave the stored commit unchanged, set to `null` to clear it, or provide a non-empty string to replace it.

gitInfo.description: Patch the stored Git metadata for this thread. Omit a field to leave it unchanged, set it to `null` to clear it, or provide a string to replace the stored value.

CollaborationMode.description: Collaboration mode for a Codex session.

ModeKind.description: Initial collaboration mode to use when the TUI starts.

ReasoningEffort.description: A non-empty reasoning effort value advertised by the model.

ReasoningSummary.description: A summary of the reasoning performed by the model. This can be useful for debugging and understanding the model's reasoning process. See https://platform.openai.com/docs/guides/reasoning?api-mode=responses#reasoning-summaries

oneOf.description: Option to disable reasoning summaries.

approvalPolicy.description: Override the approval policy for subsequent turns.

approvalsReviewer.description: Override where approval requests are routed for subsequent turns.

collaborationMode.description:
EXPERIMENTAL - Set a pre-set collaboration mode for subsequent turns.

For `collaboration_mode.settings.developer_instructions`, `null` means "use the built-in instructions for the selected mode".

cwd.description: Override the working directory for subsequent turns.

effort.description: Override the reasoning effort for subsequent turns.

model.description: Override the model for subsequent turns.

multiAgentMode.description: @deprecated Ignored. Use `effort: "ultra"` for proactive multi-agent behavior.

permissions.description: Select a named permissions profile id for subsequent turns. Cannot be combined with `sandboxPolicy`.

personality.description: Override the personality for subsequent turns.

sandboxPolicy.description: Override the sandbox policy for subsequent turns.

serviceTier.description: Override the service tier for subsequent turns. `null` clears the current service tier; omission leaves it unchanged.

summary.description: Override the reasoning summary for subsequent turns.

command.description: Shell command string evaluated by the thread's configured shell. Unlike `command/exec`, this intentionally preserves shell syntax such as pipes, redirects, and quoting. This runs unsandboxed with full access rather than inheriting the thread sandbox policy.

cursor.description: Opaque pagination cursor returned by a previous call.

numTurns.description:
The number of turns to drop from the end of the thread. Must be >= 1.

This only modifies the thread's history and does not revert local file changes that have been made by the agent. Clients are responsible for reverting these changes.

ancestorThreadId.description: Optional ancestor thread filter. Returns spawned descendants at any depth, excluding the ancestor itself. Mutually exclusive with `parentThreadId`.

archived.description: Optional archived filter; when set to true, only archived threads are returned. If false or null, only non-archived threads are returned.

cursor.description: Opaque pagination cursor returned by a previous call.

cwd.description: Optional cwd filter or filters; when set, only threads whose session cwd exactly matches one of these paths are returned.

limit.description: Optional page size; defaults to a reasonable server-side value.

modelProviders.description: Optional provider filter; when set, only sessions recorded under these providers are returned. When present but empty, includes all providers.

parentThreadId.description: Optional direct parent thread filter. Mutually exclusive with `ancestorThreadId`.

searchTerm.description: Optional substring filter for the extracted thread title.

sortDirection.description: Optional sort direction; defaults to descending (newest first).

sortKey.description: Optional sort key; defaults to created_at.

sourceKinds.description: Optional source filter; when set, only sessions from these source kinds are returned. When omitted or empty, defaults to interactive sources.

useStateDbOnly.description: If true, return from the state DB without scanning JSONL rollouts to repair thread metadata. Omitted or false preserves scan-and-repair behavior.

archived.description: Optional archived filter; when set to true, only archived threads are returned. If false or null, only non-archived threads are returned.

cursor.description: Opaque pagination cursor returned by a previous call.

limit.description: Optional page size; defaults to a reasonable server-side value.

searchTerm.description: Required substring/full-text query for thread search.

sortDirection.description: Optional sort direction; defaults to descending (newest first).

sortKey.description: Optional sort key; defaults to created_at.

sourceKinds.description: Optional source filter; when set, only sessions from these source kinds are returned. When omitted or empty, defaults to interactive sources.

cursor.description: Opaque pagination cursor returned by a previous call.

limit.description: Optional page size; defaults to no limit.

includeTurns.description: When true, include turns and their items from rollout history.

cursor.description: Opaque cursor to pass to the next call to continue after the last turn.

itemsView.description: How much item detail to include for each returned turn; defaults to summary.

sortDirection.description: Optional turn pagination direction; defaults to descending.

cursor.description: Opaque cursor to pass to the next call to continue after the last item.

sortDirection.description: Optional item pagination direction; defaults to ascending.

turnId.description: Optional turn id to filter by. When omitted, returns items across the thread.

items.description: Raw Responses API items to append to the thread's model-visible history.

cwds.description: When empty, defaults to the current session working directory.

forceReload.description: When true, bypass the skills cache and re-scan skills from disk.

cwds.description: When empty, defaults to the current session working directory.
Optional working directories used to discover repo marketplaces. When omitted, only home-scoped marketplaces and the official curated marketplace are considered.

marketplaceKinds.description: Optional marketplace kind filter. When omitted, only local marketplaces are queried, plus the default remote catalog when enabled by feature flag.

cwds.description: Optional working directories used to discover repo marketplaces.

installSuggestionPluginNames.description: Additional uninstalled plugin names that should be returned when present locally. This is used by mention surfaces that intentionally expose install entrypoints.

cursor.description: Opaque pagination cursor returned by a previous call.

forceRefetch.description: When true, bypass app caches and fetch the latest data from sources.

limit.description: Optional page size; defaults to a reasonable server-side value.

threadId.description: Optional thread id used to evaluate app feature gating from that thread's config.

additionalContext.description: Optional client-provided context fragments keyed by an opaque source identifier.

For `collaboration_mode.settings.developer_instructions`, `null` means "use the built-in instructions for the selected mode".

cwd.description: Override the working directory for this turn and subsequent turns.

effort.description: Override the reasoning effort for this turn and subsequent turns.

environments.description:
Optional environments for this turn and subsequent turns.

Omitted uses the thread sticky environments. Empty disables environment access for this turn. Non-empty selects the first environment as the current turn environment for this turn.

model.description: Override the model for this turn and subsequent turns.

multiAgentMode.description: @deprecated Ignored. Use `effort: "ultra"` for proactive multi-agent behavior.

permissions.description: Select a named permissions profile id for this turn and subsequent turns. Cannot be combined with `sandboxPolicy`.

personality.description: Override the personality for this turn and subsequent turns.

responsesapiClientMetadata.description:
Optional metadata to enrich Codex's ResponsesAPI turn metadata.

Entries are flattened into the JSON string sent as `client_metadata["x-codex-turn-metadata"]` on ResponsesAPI HTTP and websocket requests.

They are not sent as top-level ResponsesAPI `client_metadata` keys, and reserved keys such as `session_id`, `thread_id`, `turn_id`, and `window_id` cannot be overridden.

runtimeWorkspaceRoots.description: Replace the thread's runtime workspace roots for this turn and subsequent turns. Paths must be absolute.

sandboxPolicy.description: Override the sandbox policy for this turn and subsequent turns.

serviceTier.description: Override the service tier for this turn and subsequent turns.

summary.description: Override the reasoning summary for this turn and subsequent turns.

additionalContext.description: Optional client-provided context fragments keyed by an opaque source identifier.

expectedTurnId.description: Required active turn id precondition. The request fails when it does not match the currently active turn.

responsesapiClientMetadata.description:
Optional metadata to enrich Codex's ResponsesAPI turn metadata.

Entries are flattened into the JSON string sent as `client_metadata["x-codex-turn-metadata"]` on ResponsesAPI HTTP and websocket requests.

They are not sent as top-level ResponsesAPI `client_metadata` keys, and reserved keys such as `session_id`, `thread_id`, `turn_id`, and `window_id` cannot be overridden.

ThreadRealtimeStartTransport.description: EXPERIMENTAL - transport used by thread realtime.

sdp.description: SDP offer generated by a WebRTC RTCPeerConnection after configuring audio and the realtime events data channel.

ThreadRealtimeStartParams.title: ThreadRealtimeStartParams
ThreadRealtimeStartParams.description: EXPERIMENTAL - start a thread-scoped realtime session.

clientManagedHandoffs.description: Leaves Codex response handoffs to the client's explicit append calls instead of forwarding them automatically. Defaults to false.

codexResponseHandoffPrefix.description: Optional prefix added to automatic V1 Codex commentary sent with `conversation.handoff.append` when `codexResponsesAsItems` is not true. Final answers are sent without the prefix.

codexResponseItemPrefix.description: Optional prefix added to automatic Codex response items when `codexResponsesAsItems` is true.

codexResponsesAsItems.description: Sends automatic Codex responses as realtime conversation items instead of handoff appends.

flushTranscriptTailOnSessionEnd.description: Routes any transcript tail remaining at session end through Codex. Defaults to false. TODO: Remove this rollout knob once transcript-tail flushing is always enabled.

includeStartupContext.description: Set to false to start without Codex's startup context. Omitted or null includes it.

model.description: Overrides the configured realtime model for this session only.

outputModality.description: Selects text or audio output for the realtime session. Transport and voice stay independent so clients can choose how they connect separately from what the model emits.

version.description: Overrides the configured realtime protocol version for this session only.

ThreadRealtimeListVoicesParams.title: ThreadRealtimeListVoicesParams
ThreadRealtimeListVoicesParams.description: EXPERIMENTAL - list voices supported by thread realtime.

cursor.description: Opaque pagination cursor returned by a previous call.

includeHidden.description: When true, include models that are hidden from the default picker list.

limit.description: Optional page size; defaults to a reasonable server-side value.

cursor.description: Opaque pagination cursor returned by a previous call.

limit.description: Optional page size; defaults to a reasonable server-side value.

threadId.description: Optional loaded thread id. Pass this when showing feature state for an existing thread so enablement is computed from that thread's refreshed config, including project-local config for the thread's cwd.

cursor.description: Opaque pagination cursor returned by a previous call.

cwd.description: Optional working directory to resolve project config layers.

limit.description: Optional page size; defaults to the full result set.

enablement.description:
Process-wide runtime feature enablement keyed by canonical feature name.

Only named features are updated. Omitted features are left unchanged. Send an empty map for a no-op.

connectTimeoutMs.description: Optional WebSocket connection timeout. The server default applies when omitted.

cursor.description: Opaque pagination cursor returned by a previous call.

detail.description: Controls how much MCP inventory data to fetch for each server. Defaults to `Full` when omitted.

limit.description: Optional page size; defaults to a server-defined value.

oneOf.description: [UNSTABLE] FOR OPENAI INTERNAL USE ONLY - DO NOT USE. The access token must contain the same scopes that Codex-managed ChatGPT auth tokens have.

accessToken.description: Access token (JWT) supplied by the client. This token is used for backend API requests and email extraction.

chatgptAccountId.description: Workspace/account identifier supplied by the client.

chatgptPlanType.description:
Optional plan type supplied by the client.

When `null`, Codex attempts to derive the plan type from access-token claims. If unavailable, the plan defaults to `unknown`.

oneOf.title: ChatgptAuthTokensv2::LoginAccountParams

creditId.description: Opaque reset-credit identifier to redeem. When omitted, the backend selects the next available credit.

idempotencyKey.description: Identifies one logical reset attempt. A UUID is recommended; reuse the same value when retrying that attempt.

CommandExecTerminalSize.description: PTY size in character cells for `command/exec` PTY sessions.

CommandExecParams.title: CommandExecParams
CommandExecParams.description:
Run a standalone command (argv vector) in the server sandbox without creating a thread or turn.

The final `command/exec` response is deferred until the process exits and is sent only after all `command/exec/outputDelta` notifications for that connection have been emitted.

command.description: Command argv vector. Empty arrays are rejected.

cwd.description: Optional working directory. Defaults to the server cwd.

disableOutputCap.description:
Disable stdout/stderr capture truncation for this request.

Cannot be combined with `outputBytesCap`.

disableTimeout.description:
Disable the timeout entirely for this request.

Cannot be combined with `timeoutMs`.

env.description:
Optional environment overrides merged into the server-computed environment.

Matching names override inherited values. Set a key to `null` to unset an inherited variable.

outputBytesCap.description:
Optional per-stream stdout/stderr capture cap in bytes.

When omitted, the server default applies. Cannot be combined with `disableOutputCap`.

permissionProfile.description:
Optional active permissions profile id for this command.

Defaults to the user's configured permissions when omitted. Cannot be combined with `sandboxPolicy`.

processId.description:
Optional client-supplied, connection-scoped process id.

Required for `tty`, `streamStdin`, `streamStdoutStderr`, and follow-up `command/exec/write`, `command/exec/resize`, and `command/exec/terminate` calls. When omitted, buffered execution gets an internal id that is not exposed to the client.

sandboxPolicy.description:
Optional sandbox policy for this command.

Uses the same shape as thread/turn execution sandbox configuration and defaults to the user's configured policy when omitted. Cannot be combined with `permissionProfile`.

size.description: Optional initial PTY size in character cells. Only valid when `tty` is true.

streamStdin.description:
Allow follow-up `command/exec/write` requests to write stdin bytes.

Requires a client-supplied `processId`.

streamStdoutStderr.description:
Stream stdout/stderr via `command/exec/outputDelta` notifications.

Streamed bytes are not duplicated into the final response and require a client-supplied `processId`.

timeoutMs.description:
Optional timeout in milliseconds.

When omitted, the server default applies. Cannot be combined with `disableTimeout`.

tty.description:
Enable PTY mode.

This implies `streamStdin` and `streamStdoutStderr`.

CommandExecWriteParams.title: CommandExecWriteParams
CommandExecWriteParams.description: Write stdin bytes to a running `command/exec` session, close stdin, or both.

closeStdin.description: Close stdin after writing `deltaBase64`, if present.

deltaBase64.description: Optional base64-encoded stdin bytes to write.

processId.description: Client-supplied, connection-scoped `processId` from the original `command/exec` request.
Client-supplied, connection-scoped `processId` from the original `command/exec` request.
Client-supplied, connection-scoped `processId` from the original `command/exec` request.

size.description: New PTY size in character cells.

ProcessTerminalSize.description: PTY size in character cells for `process/spawn` PTY sessions.

ProcessSpawnParams.title: ProcessSpawnParams
ProcessSpawnParams.description:
Spawn a standalone process (argv vector) without a Codex sandbox on the host where the app server is running.

`process/spawn` returns after the process has started and the connection-scoped `processHandle` has been registered. Process output and exit are reported via `process/outputDelta` and `process/exited` notifications.

command.description: Command argv vector. Empty arrays are rejected.

cwd.description: Absolute working directory for the process.

env.description:
Optional environment overrides merged into the app-server process environment.

Matching names override inherited values. Set a key to `null` to unset an inherited variable.

outputBytesCap.description:
Optional per-stream stdout/stderr capture cap in bytes.

When omitted, the server default applies. Set to `null` to disable the cap.

processHandle.description:
Client-supplied, connection-scoped process handle.

Duplicate active handles are rejected on the same connection. The same handle can be reused after the prior process exits.

size.description: Optional initial PTY size in character cells. Only valid when `tty` is true.

streamStdin.description: Allow follow-up `process/writeStdin` requests to write stdin bytes.

streamStdoutStderr.description:
Stream stdout/stderr via `process/outputDelta` notifications.

Streamed bytes are not duplicated into the `process/exited` notification.

timeoutMs.description:
Optional timeout in milliseconds.

When omitted, the server default applies. Set to `null` to disable the timeout.

tty.description:
Enable PTY mode.

This implies `streamStdin` and `streamStdoutStderr`.

ProcessWriteStdinParams.title: ProcessWriteStdinParams
ProcessWriteStdinParams.description: Write stdin bytes to a running `process/spawn` session, close stdin, or both.

closeStdin.description: Close stdin after writing `deltaBase64`, if present.

deltaBase64.description: Optional base64-encoded stdin bytes to write.

size.description: New PTY size in character cells.

cwd.description: Optional working directory to resolve project config layers. If specified, return the effective config as seen from that directory (i.e., including any project layers between `cwd` and the project/repo root).

cwds.description: Zero or more working directories to include for repo-scoped detection.

includeHome.description: If true, include detection under the user's home directory.

cwd.description: Null or empty means home-scoped migration; non-empty means repo-scoped migration.

source.description: Source product that produced the migration items. Missing means unspecified.

filePath.description: Path to the config file to write; defaults to the user's `config.toml` when omitted.
Path to the config file to write; defaults to the user's `config.toml` when omitted.

reloadUserConfig.description: When true, hot-reload the updated user config into all loaded threads after writing.

refreshToken.description:
When `true`, requests a proactive token refresh before returning.

In managed auth mode this triggers the normal refresh-token flow. In external auth mode this flag is ignored. Clients should refresh tokens themselves and call `account/login/start` with `chatgptAuthTokens`.

extends.description: Parent profile identifier from the selected permissions profile's `extends` setting, when present.

id.description: Identifier from `default_permissions` or the implicit built-in default, such as `:workspace` or a user-defined `[permissions.<id>]` profile.

CodexErrorInfo.description:
This translation layer make sure that we expose codex error code in camel case.

When an upstream HTTP status is available (for example, from the Responses API or a provider), it is forwarded in `httpStatusCode` on the relevant `codexErrorInfo` variant.

oneOf.description: Failed to connect to the response SSE stream.
oneOf.title: ResponseStreamConnectionFailedCodexErrorInfo

oneOf.description: The response SSE stream disconnected in the middle of a turn before completion.
oneOf.title: ResponseStreamDisconnectedCodexErrorInfo

oneOf.description: Reached the retry limit for responses.
oneOf.title: ResponseTooManyFailedAttemptsCodexErrorInfo

oneOf.description: Returned when `turn/start` or `turn/steer` is submitted while the current active turn cannot accept same-turn steering, for example `/review` or manual `/compact`.
oneOf.title: ActiveTurnNotSteerableCodexErrorInfo

agentNickname.description: Optional random unique nickname assigned to an AgentControl-spawned sub-agent.

agentRole.description: Optional role (agent_role) assigned to an AgentControl-spawned sub-agent.

cliVersion.description: Version of the CLI that created the thread.

createdAt.description: Unix timestamp (in seconds) when the thread was created.

cwd.description: Working directory captured for the thread.

ephemeral.description: Whether the thread is ephemeral and should not be materialized on disk.

forkedFromId.description: Source thread id when this thread was created by forking another thread.

gitInfo.description: Optional Git metadata captured when the thread was created.

historyMode.description: Persisted thread history contract selected when this thread was created.

id.description: Identifier for this thread. Codex-generated thread IDs are UUIDv7.

modelProvider.description: Model provider used for this thread (for example, 'openai').

parentThreadId.description: The ID of the parent thread. This will only be set if this thread is a subagent.

path.description: [UNSTABLE] Path to the thread on disk.

preview.description: Usually the first user message in the thread, if available.

recencyAt.description: Unix timestamp (in seconds) used for thread recency ordering.

sessionId.description: Session id shared by threads that belong to the same session tree.

source.description: Origin of the thread (CLI, VSCode, codex exec, codex app-server, etc.).

status.description: Current runtime status for the thread.

threadSource.description: Optional analytics source classification for this thread.

turns.description: Only populated on `thread/resume`, `thread/rollback`, `thread/fork`, and `thread/read` (when `includeTurns` is true) responses. For all other responses and notifications returning a Thread, the turns field will be an empty list.

updatedAt.description: Unix timestamp (in seconds) when the thread was last updated.

oneOf.description: EXPERIMENTAL - proposed plan item content. The completed plan item is authoritative and may not match the concatenation of `PlanDelta` text.

aggregatedOutput.description: The command's output, aggregated from stdout and stderr.

commandActions.description: A best-effort parsing of the command to understand the action(s) it will perform. This returns a list of CommandAction objects because a single shell command may be composed of many commands piped together.

durationMs.description: The duration of the command execution in milliseconds.

processId.description: Identifier for the underlying PTY process (when available).

durationMs.description: The duration of the MCP tool call in milliseconds.

senderThreadId.description: Thread ID of the agent issuing the collab request.

status.description: Current status of the collab tool call.

tool.description: Name of the collab tool that was invoked.

durationMs.description: Duration between turn start and completion in milliseconds, if known.

error.description: Only populated when the Turn's status is failed.

id.description: Identifier for this turn. Codex-generated turn IDs are UUIDv7.

items.description: Thread items currently included in this turn payload.

itemsView.description: Describes how much of `items` has been loaded for this turn.

activePermissionProfile.description: Named or implicit built-in profile that produced the active permissions, when known.

approvalsReviewer.description: Reviewer currently used for approval requests on this thread.

instructionSources.description: Environment-native paths to instruction source files currently loaded for this thread.

runtimeWorkspaceRoots.description: Thread-scoped runtime workspace roots used to materialize `:workspace_roots`.

thread.description:
The updated thread after applying the rollback, with `turns` populated.

The ThreadItems stored in each Turn are lossy since we explicitly do not persist all agent interactions, such as command executions. This is the same behavior as `thread/resume`.

backwardsCursor.description: Opaque cursor to pass as `cursor` when reversing `sortDirection`. This is only populated when the page contains at least one thread. Use it with the opposite `sortDirection`; for timestamp sorts it anchors at the start of the page timestamp so same-second updates are not skipped.

screenshotUrls.description: Remote screenshot URLs from the plugin catalog.

name.description: Stable key used in config.toml and CLI flag toggles.

stage.description: Lifecycle stage of this feature flag.

userCode.description: One-time code the user must enter after signing in.

description.description: Backend-provided display description for this credit, or `null` when unavailable.

expiresAt.description: Unix timestamp in seconds when the credit expires, or `null` if it does not expire.

grantedAt.description: Unix timestamp in seconds when the credit was granted.

id.description: Opaque backend identifier for this reset credit.

title.description: Backend-provided display title for this credit, or `null` when unavailable.

credits.description:
Detail rows for available reset credits, when the backend provides them.

`null` means only `availableCount` is known, while an empty array means details were fetched and no available credits were returned. The backend may cap this list, so its length can be less than `availableCount`.

rateLimits.description: Backward-compatible single-bucket view; mirrors the historical payload.

rateLimitsByLimitId.description: Multi-bucket view keyed by metered `limit_id` (for example, `codex`).

oneOf.description: A reset credit was consumed and the eligible rate-limit windows were reset.
No current rate-limit window is eligible for a reset.
The account has no earned reset credits available.
The same idempotency key already completed a reset successfully.

archivedAt.description: Unix timestamp (in seconds) when the message was archived.

createdAt.description: Unix timestamp (in seconds) when the message was created.

featureEnabled.description: Whether the workspace-message backend route is available for this client.

messages.description: Active workspace messages returned by the backend.

stderr.description:
Buffered stderr capture.

Empty when stderr was streamed via `command/exec/outputDelta`.

stdout.description:
Buffered stdout capture.

Empty when stdout was streamed via `command/exec/outputDelta`.

oneOf.description: Count the full active context against the limit.
Count sampled output and later growth after the carried window prefix.

requirements.description: Null if no requirements are configured (e.g. no requirements.toml/MDM entries).

SkillsChangedNotification.title: SkillsChangedNotification
SkillsChangedNotification.description:
Notification emitted when watched local skill files change.

Treat this as an invalidation signal and re-run `skills/list` with the client's current parameters when refreshed skill metadata is needed.

TurnDiffUpdatedNotification.title: TurnDiffUpdatedNotification
TurnDiffUpdatedNotification.description: Notification that the turn-level unified diff has changed. Contains the latest aggregated diff across all file changes in the turn.

startedAtMs.description: Unix timestamp (in milliseconds) when this item lifecycle started.

read.description: This will be removed in favor of `entries`.

write.description: This will be removed in favor of `entries`.

GuardianApprovalReview.description: [UNSTABLE] Temporary approval auto-review payload used by `item/autoApprovalReview/*` notifications. This shape is expected to change soon.

GuardianApprovalReviewStatus.description: [UNSTABLE] Lifecycle state for an approval auto-review.

GuardianRiskLevel.description: [UNSTABLE] Risk level assigned by approval auto-review.

GuardianUserAuthorization.description: [UNSTABLE] Authorization level assigned by approval auto-review.

ItemGuardianApprovalReviewStartedNotific.title: ItemGuardianApprovalReviewStartedNotification
ItemGuardianApprovalReviewStartedNotific.description: [UNSTABLE] Temporary notification payload for approval auto-review. This shape is expected to change soon.

startedAtMs.description: Unix timestamp (in milliseconds) when this review started.

targetItemId.description:
Identifier for the reviewed item or tool call when one exists.

In most cases, one review maps to one target item. The exceptions are - execve reviews, where a single command may contain multiple execve calls to review (only possible when using the shell_zsh_fork feature) - network policy reviews, where there is no target item

A network call is triggered by a CommandExecution item, so having a target_item_id set to the CommandExecution item would be misleading because the review is about the network call, not the command execution. Therefore, target_item_id is set to None for network policy reviews.

AutoReviewDecisionSource.description: [UNSTABLE] Source that produced a terminal approval auto-review decision.

ItemGuardianApprovalReviewCompletedNotif.title: ItemGuardianApprovalReviewCompletedNotification
ItemGuardianApprovalReviewCompletedNotif.description: [UNSTABLE] Temporary notification payload for approval auto-review. This shape is expected to change soon.

completedAtMs.description: Unix timestamp (in milliseconds) when this review completed.

startedAtMs.description: Unix timestamp (in milliseconds) when this review started.

targetItemId.description:
Identifier for the reviewed item or tool call when one exists.

In most cases, one review maps to one target item. The exceptions are - execve reviews, where a single command may contain multiple execve calls to review (only possible when using the shell_zsh_fork feature) - network policy reviews, where there is no target item

A network call is triggered by a CommandExecution item, so having a target_item_id set to the CommandExecution item would be misleading because the review is about the network call, not the command execution. Therefore, target_item_id is set to None for network policy reviews.

completedAtMs.description: Unix timestamp (in milliseconds) when this item lifecycle completed.

PlanDeltaNotification.title: PlanDeltaNotification
PlanDeltaNotification.description: EXPERIMENTAL - proposed plan streaming deltas for plan items. Clients should not assume concatenated deltas match the completed plan item content.

oneOf.description: stdout stream. PTY mode multiplexes terminal output here.
stderr stream.

CommandExecOutputDeltaNotification.title: CommandExecOutputDeltaNotification
CommandExecOutputDeltaNotification.description:
Base64-encoded output chunk emitted for a streaming `command/exec` request.

These notifications are connection-scoped. If the originating connection closes, the server terminates the process.

capReached.description: `true` on the final streamed chunk for a stream when `outputBytesCap` truncated later output on that stream.

processId.description: Client-supplied, connection-scoped `processId` from the original `command/exec` request.

oneOf.description: stdout stream. PTY mode multiplexes terminal output here.
stderr stream.

ProcessOutputDeltaNotification.title: ProcessOutputDeltaNotification
ProcessOutputDeltaNotification.description: Base64-encoded output chunk emitted for a streaming `process/spawn` request.

capReached.description: True on the final streamed chunk for this stream when output was truncated by `outputBytesCap`.

stream.description: Output stream this chunk belongs to.

stderr.description:
Buffered stderr capture.

Empty when stderr was streamed via `process/outputDelta`.

stderrCapReached.description:
Whether stderr reached `outputBytesCap`.

In streaming mode, stderr is empty and cap state is also reported on the final stderr `process/outputDelta` notification.

stdout.description:
Buffered stdout capture.

Empty when stdout was streamed via `process/outputDelta`.

stdoutCapReached.description:
Whether stdout reached `outputBytesCap`.

In streaming mode, stdout is empty and cap state is also reported on the final stdout `process/outputDelta` notification.

FileChangeOutputDeltaNotification.title: FileChangeOutputDeltaNotification
FileChangeOutputDeltaNotification.description:
Deprecated legacy notification for `apply_patch` textual output.

The server no longer emits this notification.

oneOf.description: OpenAI API key provided by the caller and stored by Codex.
ChatGPT OAuth managed by Codex (tokens persisted and refreshed by Codex).

[UNSTABLE] FOR OPENAI INTERNAL USE ONLY - DO NOT USE.

ChatGPT auth tokens are supplied by an external host app and are only stored in memory. Token refresh must be handled by the external host app.
Backend auth supplied as request headers.
Programmatic Codex auth backed by a registered Agent Identity.
Programmatic Codex auth backed by a personal access token.
Amazon Bedrock bearer token managed by Codex.

AccountRateLimitsUpdatedNotification.title: AccountRateLimitsUpdatedNotification
AccountRateLimitsUpdatedNotification.description:
Sparse rolling rate-limit update.

Clients should merge available values into the most recent `account/rateLimits/read` response or refetch that snapshot. Nullable account metadata may be unavailable in a rolling update and does not clear a previously observed value.

AppListUpdatedNotification.title: AppListUpdatedNotification
AppListUpdatedNotification.description: EXPERIMENTAL - notification emitted when the app list changes.

RemoteControlStatusChangedNotification.title: RemoteControlStatusChangedNotification
RemoteControlStatusChangedNotification.description: Current remote-control connection status and remote identity exposed to clients.

FsChangedNotification.title: FsChangedNotification
FsChangedNotification.description: Filesystem watch notification emitted for `fs/watch` subscribers.

changedPaths.description: File or directory paths associated with this event.

message.description: Concise warning message for the user.

threadId.description: Optional thread target when the warning applies to a specific thread.

message.description: Concise guardian warning message for the user.

threadId.description: Thread target for the guardian warning.

details.description: Optional extra guidance, such as migration steps or rationale.

summary.description: Concise summary of what is deprecated.

details.description: Optional extra guidance or error details.

path.description: Optional path to the config file that triggered the warning.

range.description: Optional range for the error location inside the config file.

ThreadRealtimeStartedNotification.title: ThreadRealtimeStartedNotification
ThreadRealtimeStartedNotification.description: EXPERIMENTAL - emitted when thread realtime startup is accepted.

ThreadRealtimeItemAddedNotification.title: ThreadRealtimeItemAddedNotification
ThreadRealtimeItemAddedNotification.description: EXPERIMENTAL - raw non-audio thread realtime item emitted by the backend.

ThreadRealtimeTranscriptDeltaNotificatio.title: ThreadRealtimeTranscriptDeltaNotification
ThreadRealtimeTranscriptDeltaNotificatio.description: EXPERIMENTAL - flat transcript delta emitted whenever realtime transcript text changes.

delta.description: Live transcript delta from the realtime event.

ThreadRealtimeTranscriptDoneNotification.title: ThreadRealtimeTranscriptDoneNotification
ThreadRealtimeTranscriptDoneNotification.description: EXPERIMENTAL - final transcript text emitted when realtime completes a transcript part.

text.description: Final complete text for the transcript part.

ThreadRealtimeOutputAudioDeltaNotificati.title: ThreadRealtimeOutputAudioDeltaNotification
ThreadRealtimeOutputAudioDeltaNotificati.description: EXPERIMENTAL - streamed output audio emitted by thread realtime.

ThreadRealtimeSdpNotification.title: ThreadRealtimeSdpNotification
ThreadRealtimeSdpNotification.description: EXPERIMENTAL - emitted with the remote SDP for a WebRTC realtime session.

ThreadRealtimeErrorNotification.title: ThreadRealtimeErrorNotification
ThreadRealtimeErrorNotification.description: EXPERIMENTAL - emitted when thread realtime encounters an error.

ThreadRealtimeClosedNotification.title: ThreadRealtimeClosedNotification
ThreadRealtimeClosedNotification.description: EXPERIMENTAL - emitted when thread realtime transport closes.

AbsolutePathBuf.description:
A path that is guaranteed to be absolute and normalized (though it is not guaranteed to be canonicalized or exist on the filesystem).

IMPORTANT: When deserializing an `AbsolutePathBuf`, a base path must be set using [AbsolutePathBufGuard::new]. If no base path is set, the deserialization will fail unless the path being deserialized is already absolute.

codexHome.description: Absolute path to the server's $CODEX_HOME directory.

platformFamily.description: Platform family for the running app-server target, for example `"unix"` or `"windows"`.

platformOs.description: Operating system for the running app-server target, for example `"macos"`, `"linux"`, or `"windows"`.

oneOf.description: User approved the file changes.
User approved the file changes and future changes to the same files should run without prompting.
User denied the file changes. The agent will continue the turn.
User denied the file changes. The turn will also be immediately interrupted.

ToolRequestUserInputAnswer.description: EXPERIMENTAL. Captures a user's answer to a request_user_input question.

ToolRequestUserInputResponse.title: ToolRequestUserInputResponse
ToolRequestUserInputResponse.description: EXPERIMENTAL. Response payload mapping question ids to answers.

_meta.description: Optional client metadata for form-mode action handling.

content.description:
Structured user input for accepted elicitations, mirroring RMCP `CreateElicitationResult`.

This is nullable because decline/cancel responses have no content.

strictAutoReview.description: Review every subsequent command in this turn before normal sandboxed execution.

currentTimeAt.description: Current time as whole Unix seconds.

ReviewDecision.description: User's decision in response to an ExecApprovalRequest.

oneOf.description: User has approved this command and the agent should execute it.
User has approved this command and wants to apply the proposed execpolicy amendment so future matching commands are permitted.
oneOf.title: ApprovedExecpolicyAmendmentReviewDecision

oneOf.description: User has approved this request and wants future prompts in the same session-scoped approval cache to be automatically approved for the remainder of the session.
User chose to persist a network policy rule (allow/deny) for future requests to the same host.
oneOf.title: NetworkPolicyAmendmentReviewDecision

oneOf.description: User has denied this command and the agent should not execute it, but it should continue the session and try something else.
Automatic approval review timed out before reaching a decision.
User has denied this command and the agent should not do anything until the user's next command.
